headline: Ancient Egypt
description: Ancient Egypt (~3100 BCE - 30 BCE) was one of the world's earliest and longest-lasting civilizations along the Nile River. Known for: pyramids (Giza, ~2500 BCE), hieroglyphic writing, mummification, pharaohs as divine rulers, and remarkable stability. The regular Nile floods enabled predictable, abundant agriculture.
